intent: Stop `makepkg -i` (and thus ~/todo/install_arch.sh) from hanging forever on an orphaned /var/lib/pacman/db.lck. Vendored makepkg's run_pacman() waits on file-existence only, with no timeout, before pacman is ever called, so the existing pacman wrapper's stale-lock cleanup never runs. Fix system-wide by wrapping makepkg, mirroring the pacman-wrapper pattern.
scope: linux_configuration/scripts/periodic_background/digital_wellbeing/pacman/ (new: pacman_lock_lib.sh, makepkg_wrapper.sh, install_makepkg_wrapper.sh, rewrap_pkg_managers.sh, 96-restore-pkg-wrappers.hook; edit: pacman_wrapper.sh, install_pacman_wrapper.sh, README_FOR_LLM.md)
Non-goals: editing vendored /usr/bin/makepkg; changing pacman policy/challenge behaviour
changes: Extracted stale-lock helpers into shared pacman_lock_lib.sh (single source of truth); pacman_wrapper.sh now sources it AFTER integrity verification, and the lib is added to the SHA256 integrity manifest.

New makepkg_wrapper.sh: for install-bound invocations clears an orphaned lock (best-effort) then execs real makepkg; bypasses on FAKEROOTKEY/non-install; FAILS OPEN if lib missing so it can never brick makepkg.
Added cross-user pacman_process_running() (pgrep -x) guard: unprivileged makepkg can still detect a root pacman that its own fuser cannot see, preventing wrongful removal of a live lock.

Upgrade survival: install_makepkg_wrapper.sh + rewrap_pkg_managers.sh + PostTransaction hook (Install/Upgrade only); drift verifier check_makepkg_wrapper() in check_and_enable_services.sh; both wrappers wired into the hourly periodic driver.

Fixed pre-existing reorg path bugs: stale ~/linux-configuration path in the periodic driver, $CONFIG_DIR/hosts -> $CONFIG_DIR/scripts/periodic_background/hosts in setup + check scripts, and the test's WRAPPER_DIR path.
Fixed .orig-staleness bug in both installers (refresh backup only when target is not our symlink).

verification.evidence: test_makepkg_wrapper.sh: 18 passed, 0 failed. test_pacman_wrapper_security.sh: All Tests Passed. shellcheck --severity=warning clean on all shell files.

verification.command: deploy + real-system: sudo touch db.lck; makepkg -i --noconfirm
verification.evidence: Deployed wrapper cleared orphaned lock ('Stale pacman lock detected... Removing it automatically') and ran real makepkg; a running root 'pacman' process was respected (lock kept). sudo pacman -S licenses (real txn) rc=0 after hook Operation fix.

risks: Wrapper sits in front of makepkg system-wide; mitigated by fail-open source guard + isolation/real-system tests.
A rare hung/zombie process literally named 'pacman' can block stale-lock removal (safety over a rare re-hang).
pacman-git upgrade briefly clobbers the symlink until the PostTransaction hook/drift verifier restores it.
rollback: Restore stock binaries: sudo cp -f /usr/bin/pacman.orig /usr/bin/pacman; sudo cp -f /usr/bin/makepkg.orig /usr/bin/makepkg; sudo rm -f /etc/pacman.d/hooks/96-restore-pkg-wrappers.hook
Validate after rollback: 'pacman -Q' and 'makepkg --version' work; a real 'sudo pacman -S --noconfirm licenses' transaction completes with no hook errors.
